The automated bed and breakfast service which allows clients to move shares from their dealing accounts to their ISA accounts has been suspended until further notice.The excuse is that many of HL staff are home working so I was advised to sell and reinvest the proceeds in.my ISA account.This suggestion misses the point of a bed and breakfast transaction which is to maintain ownership of the share and avoid CGT liability. I question also why staff are being allowed to be less productive as a result of home working.This is another example of poor customer service or rather a lack of it on HL' s part whilst charging more than any of its rivals.

I did the insurance Inside this website. I founded a good price but unfortunately the insurance companies reduced the price but grow up the fees if you leave them before the expire date. Brightside is one of this. If you used the monthly payments and you want leave them before the expire date (for example if you sold the car) they will be apply full amount of fees plus another £60 and they deducted the last amount (£60) from the full amount. In few words a FRAUD. That is a example that you found in the contract: "If you decide to cancel your insurance policy outside of the cooling off period, the insurer will reclaim a proportion of commission back from us. We will apply a fee equivalent to that amount and deduct it from any returned premium from the insurer, or add it to any outstanding balance due on your premium finance." This is what I signed and unfortunately I will be pay. Read carefully before signing.

Very easy to set up, my LISA transfer from another provider was a bit slow but otherwise painless. I also opened a S&S ISA with no knowledge of the stock market, very easy and simple and you can select your risk level out of 3 levels (although I can't remember what they were called). you can see your savings grow and each account has a breakdown. Only gripe is that you can't wire money to invest/save, you have to manually input how much you want to save in the app (although you can set up auto/monthly payments) and it is taken via direct debit and takes approx 1-2 weeks from the point you add the money on the app before it appears in your balance, only issue with this is that if you do this say 1 week before payday, the amount won't come out until after payday so can mess a bit with finances. The payday boost is great as you can set a monthly amount to invest/save, only issue with this is that you can only set this up for 1 account therefore I have to manually add money into my other ISA. Overall a great app but it would be nice if you can wire money so it appears instantly rather than wait 1-2 weeks for it to process.

Further digging they are part of a cartel of insurance companies and they use the data for all sorts of analytic and pricing purposes. Do not use them, or if you do put a false name and a close address in until you are happy with the quote. Only after you have got a quote that you are satisfied with should you use genuine data. I've been with this platform for years when it was TDW but most recently as iii. I was reasonably happy with the service until I came to transfer my ISA out to another provider. The process is slow and I am being told it could take up to 5 weeks to transfer the cash value (approx. £180k). I have another ISA with Share Centre (which they now own) and that has been fairly swift. I requested at the same time. Smacks of iii hanging on to the money deliberately. I have sent two emails now asking why this is taking so long but no replies from iii. Also if I want to close my account I only get access to my online documents for 6 weeks. This is appalling. The access to these important documents needed for tax purposes etc should be allowed well in excess of this. This again is also possible on other platforms. I am not likely to be coming back to iii. Not impressed.

Since changing the rules (for the third time in six months) I can no longer sell shares if the value is below £2. So if I hold a single share below £2 it can't be sold. I have been asking them to sell the shares using the in messaging system and no one will authorize but the software won't let me sell. The charts don't load properly and take a few minutes to fix themselves. Which has given me a heart attack more than once. The ISA wrapper is a little pricy and if you do intend to hold on long term to the shares that is a excellent way to protect your profits. Here it costs £36 a year. You can get it for free on other platforms. I think there are better value platforms and the fact that I am meant to hold my shares in a company that requires frequent crowdfunding to keep afloat is unsettling to me.

These guys misled me into funding my account. I received no less than three emails informing (and congratulating) me that my account application had been 'approved'. On that basis, I transferred a not insignificant sum. The problems started when I went to make my first trade, but received an error message saying that my residential address was still being verified. Surely, if my account was 'approved', that meant that all checks were complete and my account was ready to use? Apparently not with IB! I would not have funded the account had I not been led to think that everything was good to go. So, on to the customer support, which is really, really, really, slow and unhelpful. After an hour-long live chat with excruciating waits in between responses, I was told that someone would be contacting me by email soon to help me get it resolved ASAP. I accepted that, but, still annoyed, filed my own complaint ticket as well. 24 hours later, I had received neither an email from an IB representative (aside from another generic one reminding me that my account was now 'approved' and funded!), nor a response to my complaint ticket. Having lost faith in IB and wanting to take my business elsewhere, I tried to withdraw my funds and close my account, only to be told that I must wait three days before I can do so. Given that I was misled into sending the funds in the first place, I think this is absolutely shocking. IB may be one of the cheaper brokers, but it is clearly a case of you get what you pay for. Avoid them at all costs.

I went to the link they provided in their letter and it was non existent. Long story my issue had to be bumped a few times to get to correct department that could fix the issue. IB was great when it was pretty much the only game in town, but times have changed and IB hasn't. What I didn't like is that they started charging data fees while other brokers included it. I love this app, so way to use and I have had none of the problems reported by others. Although not necessary I would suggest that you at least understand the basics of investing before using and sign up to the community. Pay attention to costs and fees that there are surrounding ETF's, do your own company research and consider other ISA investment platforms if you are a low level user but other than that I was up and running in no time at all. My only issue is no quick link to the community which is a great source of information and a place to while away the time waiting for a result. Certainly not for the gamers, gamblers and Warren Buffets of this world if you want the stress of sweating on a share price, but then if you haven't assessed your risk levels you shouldn't really be investing in the first place.

DO NOT USE THIS COMPARISON SITE! This site provides quote for companies that have no interest in their customers and people who offer low premiums but have no cover and every term and condition to help them get out of paying a claim. Do not trust the insurance companies they provide. QA Choice Insurance/ XS Direct: This company has caused me nothing but trouble and put me in the worst financial difficulty. I was a named driver on a car i got through my work for my mum. I had a seriously bad accident in the vehicle and it got written off. I made a claim and the company took 3 weeks to make a decision to void the insurance due to the V5 being in my name (the named driver) instead of my mums name (the main driver). This was an unintentional accident made when setting up the insurance and the company were not interest in the trouble this had caused us at all. Rude staff and no understanding for their customers. The company then changed us £300 to cancel the account as they voided the insurance, £1500 for storage of the vehicle when they had taken 3 weeks to make the decision to void and £1200 for the inspector who came to my house to interview myself and mum after the incident. The company refused to give us back our £3800 premium and yet still charged us £3000 charges. This has left us in £24,000 debt and a void on our insurance policy. This company will do everything in its power to not support their customers and get out of paying any claim. AVOID AT ALL TIMES!

My mom has a lot of health problems and disabilities and has struggled for far too long with the dreaded trip to do the weekly shop. I've been telling her for years to do it online, but she found it too complicated. so now I go through the app with her once a week and order her shopping online and it's been a God send for her to not have to go shopping. It's delivered to the door at a time that suits her, it's been really good for her. Only downside now is there's no longer an option for carrier bags for the shopping which might not seem much, but the baskets the shopping is delivered in are very heavy even to go from door to kitchen and the bags made it a lot easier, so now we have to get it delivered at a time when the grandkids will be there to help unpack it all to make it easier on her
